violations.claim: [NEW] runExplain – line 327 – reachable when the argument lacks a ':' separator
violations.smt2:
; [NEW] runExplain – line 327 – reachable when the argument lacks a ':' separator
; After the first guard passes, the code does:
;   const [filePart, linePart] = target.split(":");
;   if (!filePart || !linePart) { console.error(...); }
; The error is triggered when `linePart` is missing (i.e., the split yields only one piece).
; We model a concrete `target` that does not contain a colon.
; Expected result: sat (the error is reachable).

(declare-const target String)          ; the raw argument string (simplified as an uninterpreted constant)
(declare-const has_colon Int)          ; 1 if target contains ':', 0 otherwise
(declare-const filePart_defined Int)   ; 1 if filePart is non‑empty, 0 otherwise
(declare-const linePart_defined Int)   ; 1 if linePart is non‑empty, 0 otherwise

; The code: const [filePart, linePart] = target.split(":");
; If target does not contain ':', split returns an array of length 1.
; We model that situation:
(assert (= has_colon 0))

; Consequently, filePart is the whole target, linePart is undefined.
(assert (= filePart_defined 1))   ; filePart exists (non‑empty string)
(assert (= linePart_defined 0))   ; linePart is missing

; Guard that triggers the error:
; if (!filePart || !linePart) → true because linePart is missing.
(assert (or (= filePart_defined 0) (= linePart_defined 0)))

; No further constraints are needed; the above reflects an actual execution path.
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat
